methods.description: Process for selecting and collecting granodioritic glacial erosion surfaces and moraine boulders for 10 Be surface exposure dating to cover a wide range of glacial phases.

steps.description: Granodioritic glacial erosion surfaces and moraine boulders were selected for 10 Be surface exposure dating to cover the widest possible range of glacial phases.
steps.searchable_summary: select granodioritic glacial erosion surfaces and moraine boulders for 10 Be dating

steps.description: To minimise the risks of shielding by till, soil cover, or snow, we chose the highest and most prominent roches moutonnées located far from areas covered by till.
steps.searchable_summary: choose prominent roches moutonnées to minimize shielding risks

steps.description: Only boulders well anchored in till were selected. From these, boulders protruding more than 1 m over surrounding till were favoured.
steps.searchable_summary: select well-anchored boulders protruding over 1 m

steps.description: We selected up to three samples per moraine whenever suitable blocks were available. Only the two upper centimetres from selected rock surfaces were sampled.
steps.searchable_summary: select up to three samples per moraine and sample top 2 cm

methods.description: Process for extracting and preparing quartz from rock samples for 10 Be analysis.

steps.description: Samples were crushed and sieved to extract the 250-1000 μm granulometric quartz fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: crush and sieve samples to extract 250-1000 μm quartz

steps.description: Minerals other than quartz were dissolved by HCl and H2SiF6, and remaining quartz grains were cleaned using sequential HF dissolutions to remove any potential atmospheric 10 Be.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve non-quartz minerals and clean quartz grains

steps.description: Between 10 to 30 g per sample of clean quartz cores were then completely dissolved in HF and spiked with 300 μg of 9 Be carrier.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve quartz cores in HF and spike with 9 Be carrier

steps.description: Beryllium was separated by successive solvent extractions and alkaline precipitations.
steps.searchable_summary: separate beryllium by solvent extraction and precipitation

methods.description: Process for measuring 10 Be concentrations and normalizing them to ICN standards.

steps.description: AMS measurements were performed with reference to NIST Standard Reference Material 4325 using the certified 10 Be to 9 Be ratio.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS measurements using NIST standard

steps.description: We normalized the measured concentrations based on the NIST standard to ICN standards by increasing them by a factor of 1.143.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize 10 Be concentrations to ICN standards

steps.description: Analytical uncertainties were calculated based on counting statistics, a conservative estimate of 3% instrumental variability and a 50% uncertainty in the chemical blank correction.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate analytical uncertainties for 10 Be measurements

methods.description: Calculation of 10 Be production rates using various scaling methods and correction factors.

steps.description: Recalculated a SLHL production of 5.41±0.17 at g -1 y -1 quartz using the corrected Köfels-derived SLHL 10 Be production value.
Applied the scaling method of Stone (2000) to deduce the exposure ages.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Stone (2000) scaling method for exposure age calculation

steps.description: Reviewed limitations of scaling criteria and provided a more reliable scaling procedure based on mass shielding depth and effective vertical cutoff rigidity.
steps.searchable_summary: review and apply reliable scaling procedure for 10 Be production
